subject: Chemistry
title: PFAS: Forever Chemicals in Your Blood
description: A quick win: understand PFAS: Forever Chemicals in Your Blood.

hook.text: PFAS chemicals are in 98% of Americans' blood. They're called 'forever chemicals' because they virtually never break down.

buildup.text: PFAS (per- and polyfluoroalkyl substances) have carbon-fluorine bonds—the strongest bond in organic chemistry.

discovery.text: They repel water and oil, making them ideal for non-stick pans, waterproof clothing, and firefighting foam.

twist.text: PFAS accumulate in the body and are linked to cancer, thyroid disease, and immune suppression. DuPont hid the data for decades.

climax.text: The EPA set near-zero limits for PFAS in drinking water in 2024. Cleanup will cost an estimated $400 billion.

punchline.text: Chemistry's strongest bond made the hardest pollution to clean. Forever means forever.

quiz.question: Why are PFAS called 'forever chemicals'?
options: Their carbon-fluorine bonds are nearly unbreakable in the environment
They glow permanently under UV light
They are radioactive with extremely long half-lives
